mapping.a_element: HCN<->CNH isomerization as transitions between two states/wells
mapping.b_element: polymer tumbling as flips between two orientation states
universal exponential tail of the tumbling-time PDF

mapping.a_element: total energy E as microcanonical control parameter
mapping.b_element: Weissenberg number Wi as control parameter

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is a generic statistical signature: an exponential tail in a distribution of event times signalling memoryless (Poisson) escape from a two-state system. The generating machinery differs fundamentally: paper A rests on deterministic Hamiltonian transition-state theory with phase-space dividing surfaces and gap-time flux (its core objects, e.g. the dividing surface and nonstatistical power-law trapping, have no counterpart in B), whereas paper B is a thermally-driven stochastic Langevin process in an external shear flow with Wi-scaling. No shared governing equation or process maps role-for-role, so this is shared vocabulary/phenomenology, not shared machinery.
